last_item:
Band-2.5 (resource/config safety) — closed the LAST open band-2.5 item: the audit-found LOW `Manager.IsRunning` wrapper-lie follow-up. `IsRunning` (internal/pg/manager.go) judged 'is Postgres up?' by `systemctl is-active postgresql`, but on the apt-provisioned Debian/Ubuntu platform that unit is a oneshot wrapper (ExecStart=/bin/true, RemainAfterExit, non-binding Wants on the real postgresql@<ver>-main.service) that reports 'active' even when the cluster failed to start — so a down cluster could show as 'running' on the dashboard health badge (handlers_dashboard.go:63, the sole caller, `running, _ :=`). FIX (test-first): IsRunning now reuses the existing `confirmAcceptingConnections` helper (internal/pg/safeconfig.go) — a real `SELECT 1` over the local socket as the postgres OS user, bounded 30s — as the authoritative liveness signal, the SAME probe restartWithRollback already uses for its initial+post-rollback restarts (this is the third and final code path hardened against the wrapper lie, closing the learnings.md note). A probe failure (down/unreachable/up-but-hung) collapses to a clean `(false, nil)`, preserving the documented 'not running is a normal queryable state' contract; the caller already discards the error so no caller change. The body shrank to a nil-runner guard + the probe (removed the systemctl is-active call, exit-code parsing, strings.TrimSpace). Test: new is_running_test.go `TestIsRunning_ProbesPostmasterNotSystemdWrapper` (3 subtests) — down-postmaster reports not-running even with an `is-active`-says-'active' responder registered (proven RED against old code: returned true), running when the probe succeeds, requires-a-runner; asserts the probe runs AsUser=postgres and (reviewer-hardened) that `systemctl is-active` is NEVER invoked. Removed the now-obsolete table-driven `TestIsRunning` in manager_test.go (it programmed is-active responses for a path that no longer exists); kept `TestIsRunning_NoRunner` for the CodeInternal error path. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): one finding — the dead `is-active` responder needed a negative assertion to have teeth — APPLIED; no semantic-mismatch/regression/coverage-loss concerns. BAND 2.5 NOW FULLY COMPLETE. _prev = Band-2 sibling-event-drop fix (the second of the two MEDIUM items iter-44's audit left open; the first was fixed iter-45). `alert/engine.go` `Evaluate` appended each rule's event AFTER its store write and did `return nil, err` on a persist failure — so ONE rule's failed `UpsertAlert` discarded every firing/recovery already computed for OTHER rules that cycle (e.g. a `disk-almost-full` critical page computed earlier in the loop), and `runTelemetryCycle` (server/background.go) then returned early before dispatching anything. Under store pressure a real critical event could go silently undelivered — defeating the alert subsystem at the worst moment. FIX (test-first): `Evaluate` now appends each event to the result slice BEFORE the best-effort `persist`; a persist failure logs the per-rule error (Error, with the rule id), accumulates into `persistErrs`, and presses on with the remaining rules instead of aborting — returning ALL computed events plus `errors.Join(persistErrs...)`. The caller `runTelemetryCycle` now treats that error as NON-FATAL: logs a Warn and dispatches the computed events anyway (a real firing must never be lost to one rule's bad store write); only a sampling error still aborts the cycle. Unpersisted state self-heals next tick (at worst a sustained breach re-notifies without cooldown gating — strictly safer than a dropped alert). Introduced a narrow `alertStore` interface (ListAlerts/UpsertAlert) so a persist failure can be injected; `*store.Store` satisfies it structurally so no caller changed (server.go still passes the concrete store). Test (non-vacuous, RED→GREEN): `TestEvaluateOneRulePersistFailureKeepsSiblingEvents` builds a `failingUpsertStore` whose failing rule sorts FIRST by name (processed before the good rule, proving the loop doesn't abort on the first error); asserts `Evaluate` returns an error AND both events (append-before-persist), the failing rule's OWN event is still dispatched, the sibling's state was actually persisted, and the failing rule's was not. Proven RED against the old code (sibling firing dropped to ""). Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no blocking issues — fix correct, test non-vacuous, no concurrency/security/regression concerns. BOTH audit-found MEDIUM items are now CLOSED. _prev = Band-1.5 backup-failed in-memory outcome signal. _SUPERSEDED_prev: Band-1.5 (data durability) — AUDIT-FOUND silent-alert fix (one of the two MEDIUM items iter-44's audit left open). The immediate critical `backup-failed` alert was derived by the telemetry collector SOLELY from the newest `backup_history` row (`enrichBackup`), but `recordBackup` (backup/manager.go) is best-effort and swallows insert errors — so a scheduled backup that FAILS while its `fail`-row insert ALSO fails (SQLite contention / disk pressure on the panel volume) left the newest row as the prior SUCCESS and the alert NEVER fired (degrading to the 26h `backup-stale` warning), defeating the north-star 'loud alert on backup failure' guarantee. FIX (test-first): the backup `Manager` now records its last outcome IN MEMORY (`lastOutcome{at, failed, valid}` under an `outcomeMu` RWMutex), set in `Backup` right after the run completes — BEFORE and independent of the best-effort store write; exposed via `Manager.LastOutcome()`. The collector gained an optional `BackupOutcomeSource` (single method, satisfied structurally by `*backup.Manager`, wired `collector.UseBackupOutcome(s.backups)` in `newServer`); `enrichBackup` now merges the newest stored row with the in-memory outcome and takes whichever is MORE RECENT to drive `LastBackupFailed`. Behavior across the five cases: failed+insert-failed → in-memory wins → STILL fires; recovery success+insert-failed → clears; normal agreement / store-newer → store stays authoritative (richer detail); no-backups-yet → stays 0 (fresh box not 'failed'); after-restart empty memory → store governs. Tests (non-vacuous): manager — failure records in-memory outcome with NO store at all, success clears, never-ran unset; collector — stale-store-success+newer-in-memory-fail STILL fires (the regression, reads 0 vs old code), in-memory recovery clears a stale stored fail, store-wins-on-tie/newer, in-memory-only fires. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): both Important findings applied — `outcomeMu` made an RWMutex with an RLock read path (matches the `mu` RWMutex convention; readers don't block the writer) + `UseBackupOutcome` documents the construct-before-goroutines ordering. The OTHER open MEDIUM item remains: alert/engine.go:104-112 sibling-event-drop on one rule's persist failure (band 2). _prev = Band-2.5 self-heal rollback liveness-probe fix.
_OLD_prev: Band-2.5 (resource/config safety) — CRITICAL self-heal fix found by a north-star audit. `restartWithRollback` (internal/pg/safeconfig.go) — the snapshot-then-restart-then-rollback primitive used by ApplyTuning (host-sized tuning) + EnsureArchiving (WAL config) — judged 'did Postgres come back up?' SOLELY by the exit code of `systemctl restart postgresql`. On apt-installed Debian/Ubuntu (the provisioned platform) that unit is a ONESHOT WRAPPER (ExecStart=/bin/true) that pulls in the real postgresql@<ver>-main.service via a NON-binding Wants, so it exits 0 even when the cluster fails to start. Bug: a bad tuning/archiving value could leave Postgres DOWN while the panel reported success and the rollback NEVER fired — defeating the 'Postgres never left down' guarantee the whole primitive exists for. FIX: health now judged by a real liveness probe — new `confirmAcceptingConnections` runs `SELECT 1` over the local socket as the postgres OS user (peer auth, bounded 30s livenessTimeout), bypassing the lying wrapper; new `restartAndConfirm` (restart + confirm) used for BOTH the initial AND post-rollback restart, so we also never falsely claim 'running' after a rollback that didn't actually bring PG up (honest CodeInternal 'down' instead). Reused the existing bare-`psql` invocation so the //go:build integration test (tuning_apply_integration_test.go — injects PGHOST/PGPORT env into every command + translates `systemctl restart postgresql`→`pg_ctl restart -w`) keeps passing unchanged. Test-first: 2 new regression tests in safeconfig_test.go (flakyLivenessRunner: systemd-exits-0-but-PG-down → still rolls back → CodeSafety; stays-down-after-rollback → CodeInternal 'down') + augmented TestRestartWithRollback_NoRollbackOnSuccess to assert the liveness probe actually runs — all 3 RED against old code, GREEN after. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no blocking issues (SELECT 1 is stricter/correct vs pg_isready; integration test unbroken; tests non-vacuous). Added a backlog follow-up: IsRunning (manager.go:251) has the same latent wrapper-lie flaw but is LOW priority (only feeds the best-effort dashboard badge; telemetry's real PG connection is the authoritative signal there). _prev = Band-3 PgBouncer pooler UI slice 3/3
_unused_prev3_band3ui: Band-3 (usability): PgBouncer pooler UI slice 3/3 — React toggle on Settings (web/src/views/Pooler.tsx, rendered after DatabaseTuning in Settings.tsx). FEATURE COMPLETE; band 3 COMPLETE. Off by default. OFF view: plain-language explainer (when you don't need a pooler), previews loopback address (host:listen_port) + host-sized pool sizing labeled by effect (PoolSettingsTable), role picker filtered to non-superuser LOGIN roles (superusers connect directly; pool reserves conns for admin). Enable gated behind ConfirmDialog stating EXACTLY what happens first (installs pgbouncer pkg, starts service on host:listen_port, routes N named roles) + reassures NO Postgres restart, NO data touched. Enable button disabled until >=1 role picked AND status.pool != null (PG reachable — matches server 409 when Applied==nil). Client sends {roles} ONLY, never max_connections (server sizes pool; forged value can't widen it). ON view: ok callout + address + live pool sizing (or 'sizing unavailable' if pool null). New api client poolerStatus()/enablePooler() + 4 TS types (PoolRecommendation/PoolerStatus/PoolerEnableRequest/PoolerEnableResult) mirroring Go JSON. Tests Pooler.test.tsx (9): enabled address+sizing, enabled-PG-unreachable, disabled role filtering, button gated on selection, gated when PG unreachable, roles-loading shows spinner NOT empty state, no-app-roles empty state, confirm copy asserts each claim + sends {roles} + onEnabled called, failed-enable surfaces error + no success signal. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): fixed Important roles-loading race (flashed 'No app roles' before list arrived -> now threads rolesLoading + spinner) + minor state-order. _prev UI slice 2/3 = enable endpoint POST /api/pooler/enable
_unused_prev2: Band-3 (usability): PgBouncer pooler UI slice 2/3 — enable endpoint POST /api/pooler/enable (internal/server/handlers_pooler.go handlePoolerEnable). Wires the already-built/tested pgbouncer.Manager.Enable orchestrator into HTTP. New pooler *pgbouncer.Manager field built in newServer over the shared OS runner (s.pg as VerifierSource, s.store as PoolerState). Under requireAuth → inherits CSRF gate for cookie flows; no exempt entry so band-1 TestEveryStateChangingEndpointRejectsCSRF route-walk auto-covers it. Input {roles, profile} ONLY — max_connections is NOT client-supplied; pool sized server-side from live s.pg.CurrentTuning().Applied.MaxConnections (security tie-break: forged value can't widen pool past what PG serves). Guard order cheap→costly: empty roles→400 (empty auth_file locks every app out), unknown profile→400 (ParseWorkloadProfile no silent mis-size), then Applied==nil (PG unreachable)→409 CodeConflict with NO side effect rather than guess-then-install. Audited enable_pooler success/failure by CODE only (never role names/verifiers; Enable itself logs role COUNT only). Tests handlers_pooler_test.go: requires-auth(401,flag-stays-off), empty-roles-400, unknown-profile-400, refuses-when-PG-unreachable-409(flag-stays-off). Full side-effecting happy path NOT HTTP-tested by deliberate convention (backups/migrate handlers same: real OSRunner, test only guard/validation) — orchestration proven in pgbouncer/enable_test.go via fake runner. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no high-confidence findings. REMAINING: slice 3/3 React UI pooler toggle.
_unused_prev: Band-3 (usability): PgBouncer pooler UI slice 1/3 — read-only status endpoint GET /api/pooler (internal/server/handlers_pooler.go), behind requireAuth, returns {enabled, host, listen_port, pool}. enabled from pgbouncer.IsEnabled (config key pooler.enabled; absent=default-off); host/listen_port the loopback target same-box apps use (new exported pgbouncer.LoopbackHost/DefaultListenPort aliasing the unexported consts, one source of truth); pool the host-sized RecommendPool(max_connections, Mixed) sizing enabling would apply so the UI labels each setting by effect. Pool best-effort via s.pg.CurrentTuning — Postgres unreachable (Applied==nil) -> Pool nil, UI says computed-at-enable-time, never a guess. NEVER mutates host state; tuning-read error non-fatal so enabled still renders. Split the old single 'UI toggle' backlog item into 3 slices (this status endpoint done; future POST enable endpoint; React UI). Tests handlers_pooler_test.go: default-off, reflects-persisted-enable (reads same key Enable writes), requires-auth (401, no SPA leak). Dropped a premature unused pooler *pgbouncer.Manager Server field (YAGNI). Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no critical/blocking; declined the one Important finding (test CurrentTuning-error branch) — currently unreachable, would need an injection seam nothing else uses.
_prev_last_item: Band-3 (usability): PgBouncer pooler enable-flow slice 7 — backend ORCHESTRATOR. internal/pgbouncer/enable.go Manager.Enable(ctx, src VerifierSource, state PoolerState, p EnableParams) (EnableResult, error) wires the six already-built primitives into one opt-in entry point. OFF by default; idempotent/re-runnable. Order: InstallPackage -> RoleVerifiers (src, satisfied by *pg.Manager) -> EnsureConfig -> EnsureUserlist -> EnableNow -> Reload ONLY when config/auth_file changed -> IsRunning verify -> persist enabled=true LAST (only once confirmed up, so the flag can't contradict a failed bring-up). Added IsEnabled (unset key=default-off) for the UI slice; PoolerState is a narrow GetConfig/SetConfig iface *store.Store satisfies (no store coupling into the package). SECURITY (reviewer-caught, fixed pre-commit): EnsureConfig BEFORE EnsureUserlist — the .ini marker guard is the flow's only ownership check, so the secret-adjacent auth_file is never written into a dir indiepg doesn't own; a foreign distro/operator pgbouncer.ini is a hard stop with no auth_file left behind. Logs role COUNT only, never names/verifiers. Tests enable_test.go via fake VerifierSource + in-memory PoolerState + temp confdir (10 Enable cases + IsEnabled). Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): both findings applied (ordering swap + bring-up-failure test hardening). REMAINING: UI pooler toggle slice.
last_result: green: gofmt clean (gofmt -l empty), go vet ./... ok, go test ./... -count=1 all pass, go test -race ./internal/pg ./internal/server clean, CGO_ENABLED=0 build ok. No web/ changes this iteration (Go-only fix). Tree clean.
notes: ITER 47 closed the LAST open band-2.5 item — the audit-found LOW `Manager.IsRunning` wrapper-lie follow-up (now probes via confirmAcceptingConnections' SELECT 1 instead of the lying `systemctl is-active`). BAND 2.5 IS NOW FULLY COMPLETE. With bands 0/1/1.5/2/2.5/3 all done, the ONLY remaining open work is BAND 4 (cosmetic Tailwind+shadcn UI redo), which prior iterations judged to conflict with the non-negotiable rules — rule 5 (YAGNI/KISS: prefer hardening, don't add deps the indie hacker didn't ask for) and rule 4 (security tie-break: a DB admin panel pulling in the Tailwind/Radix/CVA/lucide dep tree widens supply-chain surface) — and which is ALSO mechanically blocked: installing the stack needs network + a writable ~/.npm cache, but the sandbox makes ~/.npm/_cacache read-only (EROFS). The existing hand-CSS UI is already lean + fully tested. NEXT ITERATION should pick the highest-value of: (A) another north-star audit pass (audits ARE valid iterations — the last one (iter 44) surfaced 1 critical + 2 medium + 1 low, all now fixed; a fresh deep audit may find more), or (B) re-evaluate band 4 (if pursued: scaffold Tailwind+shadcn ONE view at a time each with a test; web gate MUST run from web/ — repo-root npm hits read-only ~/.npm EROFS — AND installing deps needs the sandbox disabled; stage internal/server/web/dist with any web change so the tree stays clean). Given band 4's rule-4/5 tension + mechanical block, (A) a fresh audit is likely the higher-value pick until band 4's blockers are resolved or its value is reaffirmed. --- HISTORICAL (iter 44-46): iter 44 ran a north-star audit (1 CRITICAL self-heal wrapper-lie fixed iter 44, 2 MEDIUM silent-failure items fixed iter 45+46, 1 LOW IsRunning follow-up fixed THIS iter). All audit findings now closed. --- HISTORICAL (iter 44 context): ITER 44 was an AUDIT-FOUND REGRESSION FIX, not band-4 work. Bands 0-3 are complete; the ONLY remaining band is 4 (cosmetic Tailwind+shadcn UI redo). I judged band 4 to conflict with the non-negotiable rules — rule 5 YAGNI/KISS ('prefer hardening; don't add dependencies the indie hacker didn't ask for') and rule 4 security-tie-break (a DB admin panel pulling in the Tailwind/Radix/CVA/lucide dep tree widens the supply-chain surface), and the existing hand-CSS UI is already lean + fully tested. Also a hard mechanical blocker: installing the stack needs network + a writable ~/.npm cache, but the sandbox makes ~/.npm/_cacache read-only (EROFS). So instead of band 4 I ran a north-star audit (general-purpose subagent, 4 deep dives across backup/restore, auth/HTTP, PG lifecycle, background loop) which surfaced ONE critical load-bearing defect (the systemctl-wrapper-exit-0 lie in restartWithRollback — now FIXED this iteration) plus two MEDIUM silent-failure edges still OPEN (see below). NEXT ITERATION should pick the highest-value of: (A) the two audit-found MEDIUM silent-failure items [now in backlog under band 1.5/2.5], (B) the IsRunning wrapper-lie follow-up [band 2.5, LOW], or (C) re-evaluate band 4. AUDIT-FOUND OPEN ITEMS to add/prioritise: (1) backup-failed alert can go fully silent if the `fail` row insert ALSO fails — collector.go:77-91 derives LastBackupFailed ONLY from the newest backup_history row, and recordBackup (backup/manager.go ~:473) swallows insert errors; degrades to the 26h backup-stale warning. Fix: emit the alert from the in-memory backup outcome, independent of the store write. (2) alert/engine.go:104-112 — a single rule's persist failure does `return nil, err`, discarding every firing/recovery event already computed for OTHER rules that cycle (e.g. a disk-almost-full firing computed earlier in the loop). Fix: accumulate + persist independently, or append before persisting. Audit gave bands 1 (security), restore-clobber-safety, provisioning idempotency, and background-loop survival a CLEAN bill of health. --- BAND 4 (if pursued later): scaffold Tailwind+shadcn ONE view at a time, each with a test, no regressions; web gate MUST run from web/ (repo-root npm hits read-only ~/.npm EROFS) AND installing new deps needs the sandbox disabled (network + cache write). Stage internal/server/web/dist with any web change so the tree stays clean. --- HISTORICAL: BANDS 1.5 + 2 + 2.5 + 3 COMPLETE. Band 3 closed this iteration: the LAST open band-3 item was the PgBouncer pooler UI toggle (slice 3/3, web/src/views/Pooler.tsx) — the whole opt-in pooler feature is now done end to end (pure pool math -> config/userlist render -> atomic installers -> pg_authid verifier query -> service-lifecycle primitives -> Enable orchestrator -> GET /api/pooler status -> POST /api/pooler/enable -> React toggle). Band-3 provision-flow item (a) was DROPPED iter 33 (no panel provision route — CLI install; served read-only by the tuning card). NEXT (band 4, UI redo with shadcn — migrate ONE view per iteration, each with a test, no regressions): (4a) scaffold Tailwind + shadcn into the Vite app without regressing existing views (config, base components, one pilot view migrated) — acceptance: build green, pilot view visually intact, test passes; then (4b) migrate remaining views one per iteration (Login, Dashboard, RolesDatabases, Query, Backups, Migrate, Alerts, Settings). CAUTION for band 4: the embedded SPA is served from internal/server/web/dist (committed); `cd web && npm run build` regenerates it (JS/CSS hashes change) — stage dist with the change so the tree stays clean. Web gate MUST run from web/ (repo-root npm hits a read-only ~/.npm cache, EROFS). --- HISTORICAL band-3 detail below. BANDS 1.5 + 2 + 2.5 COMPLETE; BAND 3 (usability) WAS IN PROGRESS. Provision-flow item (a) DROPPED iter 33 (no panel provision route — CLI install; served by the tuning card). The ONLY remaining band-3 feature is the OPT-IN PgBouncer pooler, now being built incrementally like RecommendTuning was. PgBouncer slice 1 DONE iter 33: pure host-sized pool math internal/pgbouncer/pool.go (RecommendPool + SettingsMap, ports sm/DEFAULTS.md: avail=max_conn-5, default_pool=int(avail*util) floor20, min=/4 floor5, reserve=/5 floor5, max_client=default*multiplex, idle 300/600; safe defaults scram/transaction/DISCARD-ALL hardcoded). NEXT band-3 sub-items (in order): (i) DONE iter 34 — pgbouncer.ini render internal/pgbouncer/config.go RenderConfig; (ii) DONE iter 35 — config installer internal/pgbouncer/install.go Manager.EnsureConfig (atomic temp+rename, owner pgbouncer 0640, marker-guarded foreign->CodeConflict, deterministic no-op, O_NOFOLLOW symlink guard); (iii-a) DONE iter 36 — SCRAM userlist render internal/pgbouncer/userlist.go RenderUserlist([]UserlistEntry) (pure render of the `"user" "verifier"` auth_file: >=1 entry, SCRAM-only/no-downgrade, verifier+username injection guards, dup-refused, deterministic sort); (iii-b) DONE iter 37 — atomic auth_file installer internal/pgbouncer/install.go Manager.EnsureUserlist(ctx, []UserlistEntry) (renders via RenderUserlist then installs userlist.txt atomically: temp+rename, 0640 owner pgbouncer, O_NOFOLLOW symlink guard, deterministic no-op; NO marker guard since the format can't carry one — ownership gated upstream by EnsureConfig's .ini marker, auth_file is a derived satellite; logs only user count; DRYed shared atomicInstall(path,data,mode) out of writeConfigFile); (iii-c) DONE iter 38 — pg_authid SCRAM verifier query internal/pg/verifiers.go Manager.RoleVerifiers(ctx, roleNames []string) ([]RoleVerifier, err): the one input EnsureUserlist/RenderUserlist still needed fed, read via the PRIVILEGED psql path (rolpassword is superuser-only) — logs nothing, ValidateIdentifier+QuoteLiteral names, missing-role NotFound, empty-rolpassword Validation (never dropped), de-dup, deterministic sort, verbatim pass-through (SCRAM gate stays in RenderUserlist); (iii-d) DONE iter 39 — service-lifecycle primitives internal/pgbouncer/service.go: InstallPackage (apt update+install -y pgbouncer, noninteractive, idempotent, update-fail-stops-install), EnableNow (systemctl enable --now, idempotent), Reload (systemctl reload/SIGHUP first — no client-conn drop — restart fallback; both-fail CodeExec; caller reloads ONLY when changed), IsRunning (is-active: reported-state→false, empty-stdout+runner-error→CodeExec so verify-after-start can't misread 'couldn't ask' as 'down'). NOT wired yet. (iii-e) <== NEXT orchestrator Enable(...): InstallPackage -> RoleVerifiers -> EnsureUserlist + EnsureConfig -> EnableNow -> Reload ONLY when config/userlist changed -> IsRunning verify; OFF by default; idempotent; persist enabled state; any restart follows sm safety conventions; (iv) UI toggle (off by default) showing the app connection string via the pooler + pool settings labeled by effect, with a confirm stating exactly what enabling does + vitest test. After these, band 3 is complete -> band 4 (UI redo, shadcn). DONE iter 32: destructive-confirm AUDIT + TESTS — all confirm sites already state consequence + irreversibility and gate destructive ops behind a typed exact-name match; was untested, so added web/src/components/ConfirmDialog.test.tsx (8 tests) locking: plain-language consequence shown, TypedConfirmDialog cannot fire onConfirm until exact name typed (also gated while busy). No component changes. Band-3 item (b) DONE. Test-only tip: web npm/npx from repo ROOT hits a read-only ~/.npm cache (EROFS) when it tries to fetch — run the web gate from web/ (npm run typecheck/build/test) or invoke web/node_modules/.bin/{vitest,tsc} directly with the project path. NEXT IS BAND 3 (usability): top open items — (a) provision flow shows computed best-defaults up front with each override labeled by effect, safe to accept blindly; (c) PgBouncer opt-in pooler toggle (DEFAULTS.md pool math, off by default, shows app connection string). NOTE the tuning UI just shipped is read-only/preview; if a real in-panel profile-switch is ever wanted it MUST funnel through ApplyTuning's restartWithRollback + a confirm dialog stating the brief restart (fits band-3 destructive-confirm item) — but it is NOT load-bearing for the north star, so only add it if usability work surfaces a genuine need. DONE iter 31: APPLY host-sized tuning UI SURFACE — read-only GET /api/tuning (Manager.CurrentTuning, internal/pg/tuning_status.go) returns detected RAM/CPU + live applied settings (from pg_settings, whole MB, Applied=nil best-effort when PG unreachable) + per-profile host-sized recommendation; 'Database tuning' card on Settings (web/src/views/DatabaseTuning.tsx) shows applied settings + a per-profile PREVIEW labeled by effect, with copy making clear a non-default selection changes nothing and a real switch needs a restart (no restart-trigger surface). DONE iter 30: APPLY host-sized tuning REAL-PG INTEGRATION TEST (internal/pg/tuning_apply_integration_test.go, //go:build integration, INDIEPG_PG_BINDIR-gated). Proves ApplyTuning end-to-end on a throwaway PG14 cluster: settings land in pg_settings (bytes via unit col, max_connections exact), 2nd run no-op, an unstartable max_connections=1 rolls back via restartWithRollback with PG up -> CodeSafety. tuningTestRunner translates the exact `systemctl restart postgresql` -> pg_ctl restart. INTEGRATION-TEST GOTCHAS: force a deterministic boot failure with a CROSS-GUC constraint (max_connections=1), NOT an oversized memory GUC (overcommit makes it hang); and pg_ctl start/restart needs -l <logfile> or the daemonized postmaster holds the captured stdout pipe and cmd.Run hangs forever. ONLY REMAINING band-2.5 item: the ApplyTuning UI SURFACE — show the applied host-sized defaults (Provision already returns recommended_tuning + tuning status) + the workload-profile override labeled by effect (oltp/olap/mixed); vitest/RTL test; off-the-default profile clearly described before it changes sizing. After that, band 2.5 is complete -> band 3 (usability). DONE iter 29: APPLY host-sized tuning at provision — Manager.ApplyTuning (internal/pg/tuning_apply.go) persists RecommendTuning's five settings via ALTER SYSTEM, idempotent vs pg_settings (bytes-normalised through the unit col), restart-requiring (shared_buffers/max_connections) funneled through restartWithRollback, reloadable via pg_reload_conf. Wired into Provision (replaces iter-28 'surfaced, not applied'); a CodeSafety rollback is tolerated gracefully (provision still succeeds on prior good config). detectTuning seam added for deterministic provision tests. Fully unit-tested. THE TWO NEW TOP BAND-2.5 ITEMS are the apply follow-ups: (a) a real-PG //go:build integration test proving settings land in pg_settings, a 2nd run no-ops, and an oversized shared_buffers rolls back with PG up; (b) the UI surface showing the applied defaults (Provision already returns recommended_tuning + tuning status) + the workload-profile override labeled by effect. ANY future config-apply/override flow MUST funnel its restart through restartWithRollback (snapshot before write) — ApplyTuning is the worked example. DONE iter 28: host-sized tuning PURE SIZING FUNCTION — RecommendTuning(memoryMB, cpuCount, profile) in internal/pg/tuning.go ports the sm tuning.py math (DEFAULTS.md) for shared_buffers/effective_cache_size/work_mem/maintenance_work_mem/max_connections; WorkloadProfile selectable, empty->Mixed default, unknown->ValidationError; total/panic-free on degenerate input. Tested vs sample RAM 1GB-128GB x all 3 profiles + clamp/ordering/degenerate/parse/SettingsMap. DONE iter 27: connection-saturation alert CRITICAL tier connections-critical (pg.connections_percent >= 95%, For 1m, Cooldown 15m, Critical) escalating the existing connections-near-max WARNING (85%); rule-only, metric already real (sampler.go pg_stat_activity / max_connections), ID-based seed auto-adds on upgrade; TestConnectionSaturationTiers proves both tiers fire non-vacuously. DONE iter 26: SELF-HEALING config primitive (internal/pg/safeconfig.go): snapshotAutoConf captures <data_directory>/postgresql.auto.conf BEFORE a restart-requiring change (fails closed if unreadable); restartWithRollback restarts PG and on non-zero `systemctl restart` exit restores the snapshot via `tee` as the postgres user (keeps owner+0600; works while PG is down, unlike ALTER SYSTEM RESET) + restarts again so PG is never left down -> CodeSafety ('rolled back; PG running') or CodeInternal ('PG down'+hint). ANY future config-apply/override flow MUST funnel its restart through restartWithRollback (snapshot before write) — this is exactly what the new APPLY item above must do. DONE iter 25: disk-headroom-low early-warning rule (WARNING 80% host.disk_percent) below critical disk-almost-full (90%). KEY: adding a disk/conn alert is usually RULE-ONLY — Snapshot already has the fields, alert/metrics.go metricValue computes the percent, and background.go's seedDefaultAlertRules inserts only missing IDs (idempotent) so a new DefaultRules() entry auto-seeds on upgrade. Test pattern: extend TestDefaultRules want-list + a focused engine test; background_test asserts len(DefaultRules()) dynamically. --- Alert plumbing: Collector.SampleOnce -> Engine.Evaluate -> dispatchAlertEvents (background.go); metricValue (alert/metrics.go) reads Snapshot fields; go is a snap -> run go/psql/pg_ctl sandbox-disabled; empty $TMPDIR in some shells -> use /tmp. --- HISTORICAL: band 1.5 (backups proven-restorable, S3 nudge, restore-test verify+deep, scheduled jobs) and band 2 (web silent-failure audit, idempotent provisioning, query-box auto-LIMIT+statement_timeout enforced) all COMPLETE. See git log.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
